title: Jurinea gilesii
taxonomy: Jurinea gilesii is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. It belongs to the genus Jurinea, which comprises approximately 80 species of plants native to the Mediterranean region. Jurinea gilesii is further classified as a member of the subfamily Carduoideae, within which it is thought to be closely related to other Jurinea species. As a member of the Asteraceae family, Jurinea gilesii shares characteristics such as composite flowers and a daisy-like appearance. The classification of Jurinea gilesii reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the family and its geographic distribution.

intro: Jurinea gilesii, also known as Giles' Jurinea, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Asteraceae family, native to the mountainous regions of China. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, making it beneficial in the treatment of various health conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Jurinea gilesii include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Jurinea gilesii is commonly used in traditional Chinese medicine and is often prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions, as well as being used in the production of extracts and capsules.
